notes.anchor: carried to you in the gazette
notes.note: Renders in actis: the acta (acta diurna), the official Roman gazette of senatorial proceedings and public news, copies of which were sent out to provincial commanders.

notes.anchor: As though Asia were sealed off
notes.note: Cicero complains of a communications blackout from the East: no firm news crosses from the province of Asia, only unconfirmed rumours of Dolabella's defeat.

notes.anchor: it is pledged to the year of your consulship
notes.note: Cassius and M. Brutus had been designated consuls for 41 BC; Cicero stakes the Republic's slender hope on that future year of their joint magistracy.
